title: Justinian I becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Justinian I, also known as Justinian the Great, was Eastern Roman emperor from 527 to 565.

title: Konstantinos XI Palaiologos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Constantine XI Dragases Palaiologos or Dragaš Palaeologus was the last reigning Byzantine emperor from 23 January 1449 until his death in battle at the fall of Constantinople on 29 May 1453.

title: Theodosius I becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Theodosius I, also known as Theodosius the Great, was Roman emperor from 379 to 395.

title: Heraclius becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Heraclius was Byzantine emperor from 610 to 641.

title: Theodora becomes Byzantine empress
fact: Theodora was Eastern Roman empress and wife of emperor Justinian I.

title: Basil II becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Basil II Porphyrogenitus, given the epithet the Bulgar Slayer, was the senior Byzantine emperor from 976 to 1025.

title: Constantius II becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Constantius II was Roman emperor from 337 to 361.

title: Alexios I Komnenos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Alexios I Komnenos, Latinized as Alexius I Comnenus, was Byzantine emperor from 1081 to 1118.

title: Irene of Athens becomes Byzantine empress
fact: Irene of Athens, surname Sarantapechaena, was Byzantine empress consort to Emperor Leo IV from 775 to 780, regent during the childhood of their son Constantine VI from 780 until 790, co-ruler from 792 until 797, and finally empress regnant and sole ruler of the Eastern Roman Empire from 797 to 802.

title: Zeno becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Zeno was Eastern Roman emperor from 474 to 475 and again from 476 to 491.

title: Basil I becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Basil I, nicknamed "the Macedonian", was Byzantine emperor from 867 to 886.

title: Michael III becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Michael III, also known as Michael the Drunkard, was Byzantine emperor from 842 to 867.

title: Theodosius II becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Theodosius II, called "the Calligrapher", was Roman emperor from 402 to 450.

title: Manuel I Komnenos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Manuel I Komnenos was a Byzantine emperor of the 12th century who reigned over a crucial turning point in the history of Byzantium and the Mediterranean.

title: Justinian II becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Justinian II, nicknamed "the Slit-Nosed", was the last Byzantine emperor of the Heraclian dynasty, reigning from 685 to 695 and again from 705 to 711.

title: Valens becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Valens was Roman emperor from 364 to 378.

title: Jovian becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Jovian was Roman emperor from June 363 to February 364.

title: Leo III the Syrian becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Leo III the Isaurian, also known as the Syrian, was the first Byzantine emperor of the Isaurian dynasty from 717 until his death in 741.

title: Maurice becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Maurice was Eastern Roman emperor from 582 to 602 and the last member of the Justinian dynasty.

title: Andronikos I Komnenos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Andronikos I Komnenos, Latinized as Andronicus I Comnenus, was Byzantine emperor from 1183 to 1185.

title: Arcadius becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Arcadius was Roman emperor from 383 to his death in 408.

title: John II Komnenos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: John II Komnenos or Comnenus was Byzantine emperor from 1118 to 1143.

title: Phocas becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Phocas was Eastern Roman emperor from 602 to 610.

title: Michael VIII Palaiologos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Michael VIII Palaiologos or Palaeologus reigned as Byzantine emperor from 1261 until his death in 1282, and previously as the co-emperor of the Empire of Nicaea from 1259 to 1261.

title: Justin I becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Justin I, also called Justin the Thracian, was Eastern Roman emperor from 518 to 527.

title: Nikephoros II Phokas becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Nikephoros II Phokas, Latinized Nicephorus II Phocas, was Byzantine emperor from 963 to 969.

title: Leo VI the Wise becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Leo VI, also known as Leo the Wise, was Byzantine Emperor from 886 to 912.

title: Konstantinos VII becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Constantine VII Porphyrogenitus was the fourth Byzantine emperor of the Macedonian dynasty, reigning from 6 June 913 to 9 November 959.

title: Justin II becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Justin II was Eastern Roman emperor from 565 until 578.

title: Andronikos II Palaiologos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Andronikos II Palaiologos, Latinized as Andronicus II Palaeologus, reigned as Byzantine emperor from 1282 to 1328.

title: Manuel II Palaiologos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Manuel II Palaiologos or Palaeologus was Byzantine emperor from 1391 to 1425.

title: Anastasius I becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Anastasius I Dicorus was Eastern Roman emperor from 491 to 518.

title: Andronikos III Palaiologos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Andronikos III Palaiologos, commonly Latinized as Andronicus III Palaeologus, was the Byzantine emperor from 1328 to 1341.

title: Konstantinos IX Monomachos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Constantine IX Monomachos reigned as Byzantine emperor from June 1042 to January 1055.

title: Marcian becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Marcian was Roman emperor of the East from 450 to 457.

title: John V Palaiologos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: John V Palaiologos or Palaeologus was Byzantine emperor from 1341 to 1391, with interruptions.

title: Leo I becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Leo I, also known as the Thracian, was Eastern Roman emperor from 457 to 474.

title: Isaac II Angelos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Isaac II Angelos or Angelus was Byzantine Emperor from 1185 to 1195, and co-Emperor with his son Alexios IV Angelos from 1203 to 1204.

title: Constans II becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Constans II, also called "the Bearded", was the Byzantine emperor from 641 to 668.

title: John I Tzimiskes becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: John I Tzimiskes was the senior Byzantine emperor from 969 to 976.

title: Zoe Porphyrogenita becomes Byzantine empress
fact: Zoe Porphyrogenita was a member of the Macedonian dynasty who briefly reigned as Byzantine empress in 1042, alongside her sister Theodora.

title: Romanos IV Diogenes becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Romanos IV Diogenes (Greek: Ῥωμανός Διογένης, romanized: Rōmanos Diogenēs; was Byzantine emperor from 1068 to 1071.

title: Konstantinos V becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Constantine V was Byzantine emperor from 741 to 775.

title: Constans becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Flavius Julius Constans, also called Constans I, was Roman emperor from 337 to 350.

title: John VIII Palaiologos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: John VIII Palaiologos was the penultimate Byzantine emperor.

title: Nikephoros I becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Nikephoros I, also known as Nicephorus I, was Byzantine emperor from 802 to 811.

title: Theophilos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Theophilos was Byzantine Emperor from 829 until his death in 842.

title: Constantine IV becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Constantine IV, called the Younger and often incorrectly the Bearded out of confusion with his father, was Byzantine emperor from 668 to 685.

title: Romanos II becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Romanos II was Byzantine Emperor from 959 to 963.

title: Tiberius II Constantine becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Tiberius II Constantine was Eastern Roman emperor from 574 to 582.

title: Konstantinos VIII becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Constantine VIII was de jure Byzantine emperor from 962 until his death.

title: Basiliscus becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Basiliscus was Eastern Roman emperor from 9 January 475 to August 476.

title: Theodora Porphyrogenita becomes Byzantine empress
fact: Theodora Porphyrogenita was Byzantine Empress from 21 April 1042 to her death on 31 August 1056, and sole ruler from 11 January 1055.

title: Michael II becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Michael II, called the Amorian and the Stammerer, reigned as Byzantine emperor from 25 December 820 to his death on 2 October 829, the first ruler of the Amorian dynasty.

title: Konstantinos VI becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Constantine VI, sometimes called the Blind, was Byzantine emperor from 780 to 797.

title: John VI Kantakouzenos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: John VI Kantakouzenos or Cantacuzene was a Byzantine Greek emperor and nobleman, statesman, and general.

title: Alexios III Angelos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Alexios III Angelos, Latinized as Alexius III Angelus, was Byzantine Emperor from March 1195 to 17/18 July 1203.

title: Leo IV the Khazar becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Leo IV the Khazar was Byzantine emperor from 775 to 780 AD.

title: Alexios II Komnenos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Alexios II Komnenos, Latinized Alexius II Comnenus, was Byzantine emperor from 1180 to 1183.

title: Leo II becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Leo II, called the Younger, briefly reigned as a child emperor of the Eastern Roman Empire from 473 to 474.

title: Leo V the Armenian becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Leo V the Armenian was the Byzantine emperor from 813 to 820.

title: Michael VII Doukas becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Michael VII Doukas or Ducas, nicknamed Parapinakes, was the senior Byzantine emperor from 1071 to 1078.

title: Alexios V Doukas becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Alexios V Doukas, Latinized as Alexius V Ducas, was Byzantine emperor from February to April 1204, just prior to the sack of Constantinople by the participants of the Fourth Crusade.

title: Michael IV the Paphlagonian becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Michael IV the Paphlagonian was Byzantine Emperor from 11 April 1034 to his death on 10 December 1041.

title: Romanos III Argyros becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Romanos III Argyros, or Argyropoulos, was Byzantine Emperor from 1028 until his death in 1034.

title: Isaac I Komnenos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Isaac I Komnenos or Comnenus was Byzantine emperor from 1057 to 1059, the first reigning member of the Komnenian dynasty.

title: Konstantinos X Doukas becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Constantine X Doukas or Ducas, was Byzantine emperor from 1059 to 1067.

title: Heraclius Constantine becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Heraclius Constantine, often enumerated as Constantine III, was one of the shortest reigning sole Byzantine emperors, ruling for three months in 641.

title: Heraklonas becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Heraclius, known by the diminutive Heraclonas or Heracleonas, and sometimes called Heraclius II, was briefly Byzantine emperor in 641.

title: Alexander becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Alexander was briefly Byzantine emperor from 912 to 913, and the third emperor of the Macedonian dynasty.

title: Theodosios III becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Theodosius III was the Byzantine emperor from c.

title: John III Doukas Vatatzes becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: John III Doukas Vatatzes, Latinized as Ducas Vatatzes, was Emperor of Nicaea from 1221 to 1254.

title: Nikephoros III Botaneiates becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Nikephoros III Botaneiates, Latinized as Nicephorus III Botaniates, was Byzantine Emperor from 7 January 1078 to 1 April 1081.

title: Alexios IV Angelos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Alexios IV Angelos, Latinized as Alexius IV Angelus, was Byzantine Emperor from August 1203 to January 1204.

title: Michael I Rangabe becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Michael I Rangabe was Byzantine emperor from 811 to 813.

title: Michael V Kalaphates becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Michael V Kalaphates was Byzantine emperor for four months in 1041–1042.

title: Theodore I Laskaris becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Theodore I Komnenos Laskaris or Lascaris was the first emperor of Nicaea—a successor state of the Byzantine Empire—from 1205 to his death.

title: Stauracius becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Staurakios or Stauracius was the shortest-reigning Byzantine emperor, ruling for 68 days between 26 July and 2 October 811.

title: Romanos I Lekapenos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Romanos I Lakapenos or Lekapenos, Latinized as Romanus I Lacapenus or Romanus I Lecapenus, was Byzantine emperor from 920 until his deposition in 944, serving as regent for and senior co-ruler of the young Constantine VII.

title: Andronikos IV Palaiologos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Andronikos IV Palaiologos or Andronicus IV Palaeologus was the eldest son of Byzantine Emperor John V Palaiologos.

title: Pulcheria becomes Byzantine empress
fact: Aelia Pulcheria was an Eastern Roman empress who advised her brother, the emperor Theodosius II, during his minority and then became wife to emperor Marcian from November 450 to her death in 453.

title: Philippikos Bardanes becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Philippicus, born Bardanes was Byzantine emperor from 711 to 713.

title: Tiberios III becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Tiberius III, born Apsimar, was Eastern Roman emperor from 698 to 705.

title: Leontius becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Leontius was Byzantine emperor under the regnal name Leo from 695 to 698.

title: Anastasios II becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Anastasius II, born Artemius, was the Byzantine emperor from 713 to 715.

title: Michael VI Bringas becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Michael VI Bringas, also called Stratiotikos and the Old, reigned as Byzantine emperor from 1056 to 1057.

title: Aelia Eudocia becomes Byzantine empress
fact: Aelia Eudocia Augusta, also called Saint Eudocia, was an Eastern Roman empress by marriage to Emperor Theodosius II.

title: John VII Palaiologos becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: John VII Palaiologos or Palaeologus was Byzantine emperor for five months in 1390, from 14 April to 17 September.

title: Aelia Eudoxia becomes Byzantine empress
fact: Aelia Eudoxia was Eastern Roman empress by marriage to the Roman emperor Arcadius.

title: Theodore II Laskaris becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Theodore II Doukas Laskaris or Ducas Lascaris was Emperor of Nicaea from 1254 to 1258.

title: Ariadne becomes Byzantine empress
fact: Aelia Ariadne was Eastern Roman empress as the wife of Zeno and Anastasius I.

title: Sophia becomes Byzantine empress
fact: Aelia Sophia was Eastern Roman empress as the wife of Emperor Justin II.

title: John IV Laskaris becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: John IV Doukas Laskaris was the fourth emperor of the Nicaean Empire from August 16, 1258 to December 25, 1261, one of the Greek successor states formed after the Sack of Constantinople by the Roman Catholics during the Fourth Crusade.

title: Konstantinos Laskaris becomes Byzantine emperor
fact: Constantine Laskaris may have been Byzantine Emperor for a few months from 1204 to early 1205.
